site.name: FuturisticBD Market Watch
site.tagline: Daily Bangladesh capital-market intelligence · price-sensitive, policy, macro & global

marketSnapshot.asOf: DSE close, Monday, June 15, 2026 (latest completed session; June 16, 2026 figures not yet published at briefing time)

items.title: DSEX hits 9.5-month high on Islami Bank-led rally; index holds above 5,600
bullets: Benchmark DSEX rose 15 points (0.27%) to 5,640 in the June 15 session, its highest close since late last year and a third straight gaining session; DS30 added 8 points to 2,128.
Turnover climbed 7% to Tk1,456 crore on stronger liquidity, but breadth stayed negative (145 advancers vs 184 decliners, 66 unchanged) as investors booked profits after the multi-month peak.
Islami Bank Bangladesh surged 10% to the upper circuit on expectations of regulatory liquidity support, topping the gainers list; ceramics (+1.9%), banking (+1.2%) and cement (+0.9%) led sectors.
Beximco Ltd was the top loser (-9.95%); general insurance dominated turnover (15.9%), then banking (13.3%) and textiles (10.1%). CSE indices also rose (CASPI +52 to 15,395).

sources.label: The Business Standard

items.title: DSE halts Sonargaon Textiles trading amid unusual price surge
bullets: The Dhaka Stock Exchange suspended trading of Sonargaon Textiles after an abnormal, unexplained run-up in its share price and volume.
The stock had been among the session's top gainers; the halt is a routine surveillance step pending a company query response on price-sensitive information.
Such interventions typically cool speculative momentum in the affected counter and signal tighter regulatory scrutiny of low-cap textile names.

sources.label: The Business Standard

items.title: Islami Bank receives another Tk2,500cr liquidity support from central bank
bullets: Bangladesh Bank extended a further Tk2,500 crore in liquidity support to Islami Bank, reinforcing confidence in the lender after its board was dissolved.
The fresh support underpinned the stock's 10% upper-circuit rally and helped lift the broader banking sector and the DSEX.
Continued state backing reduces near-term solvency concerns but keeps focus on the bank's asset quality and governance reset.

sources.label: The Business Standard

items.title: Bangladesh Bank dissolves Islami Bank board; neutral administrator appointed
bullets: The central bank dissolved Islami Bank's board and installed an administrator promising a neutral, non-political new panel and uninterrupted customer services.
The Association of Bankers, Bangladesh (ABB) welcomed the decision but warned against 'mob' interference in banking governance.
The move is a major governance intervention in the country's largest private bank, with implications for confidence across the Shariah-banking segment.

sources.label: TBS – ABB on Islami Bank board move
TBS – customers welcome dissolution

items.title: BRAC Bank declares 15% cash + 15% stock dividend on Tk9.12 EPS for 2025
bullets: BRAC Bank recommended a 15% cash and 15% stock dividend for the year ended December 31, 2025, with the AGM held June 11 and record date May 17.
Consolidated EPS came in at Tk9.12, consolidated NAV per share at Tk51.56 and NOCFPS at Tk72.72 — a strong cash-flow profile.
The healthy payout and earnings support sentiment toward large-cap private banks driving the current DSEX rally.

sources.label: StockNow – BRAC Bank

items.title: Mutual Trust Bank re-fixes AGM, record date after BSEC nod for stock dividend
bullets: Mutual Trust Bank reset its AGM and record date after the BSEC consented to its 12% stock dividend for the year ended December 31, 2025.
Regulatory approval of stock dividends has become a recurring theme this AGM season as banks conserve cash amid tight liquidity.
Record-date resets matter for entitlement; investors buying before the new record date qualify for the dividend.

sources.label: The Business Standard

items.title: LRBDL seeks to redeploy idle IPO funds; EGM set for July 9, record date June 30
bullets: LR Bangladesh (LRBDL) will ask shareholders to approve using ~Tk20 crore of idle IPO proceeds to settle outstanding LC liabilities and loan-rescheduling down payments.
The board (187th meeting, June 9) flagged eroded working capital since COVID-19, the Russia-Ukraine war and 2024 political unrest; EGM is July 9, record date June 30.
Redeploying idle IPO money signals balance-sheet stress but could ease the company's near-term liabilities if approved.

sources.label: AmarStock – DSE disclosures

items.title: Bangladesh Bank holds policy rate at 10% in inflation-vs-growth trade-off
bullets: The central bank kept its policy (repo) rate at 10%, maintaining the tight stance held since October 2024 to defend the taka and rebuild reserves.
Inflation has proven sticky, rising for a third straight month to 8.58% earlier in the year and staying above target.
High real rates support exchange-rate stability but weigh on credit growth and equity valuations outside defensive large-caps.

sources.label: The Daily Star

items.title: Forex reserves cross $29 billion under IMF (BPM6) method
bullets: Bangladesh's gross reserves rose above $29 billion (about $29.47b) on the IMF's calculation method for the first time, up from $29.23b in early February.
Replenishment has been driven by strong remittance inflows and moderated import demand over the past year.
A thicker reserve buffer reduces taka-depreciation and import-cover risk — a supportive macro backdrop for equities and importers.

sources.label: The Daily Star

items.title: Cash still accounts for 67.2% of transactions despite cashless push
bullets: Cash remains dominant at 67.2% of transactions in Bangladesh even as MFS and digital payments expand, underscoring slow cashless adoption.
The report notes the taka has lost ~94% of its value versus the US dollar over 50 years, with the kerb rate near Tk124/$1.
Persistent cash reliance limits the addressable market for fintech and digital-banking listings over the medium term.

sources.label: The Business Standard

items.title: NBR ends customs' arbitrary import valuation to ease doing business
bullets: The National Board of Revenue moved to scrap customs officers' discretionary (arbitrary) valuation of imported goods, aiming to cut clearance friction and disputes.
Separately, NBR is widening its surcharge net by requiring inherited assets to be valued in tax files.
Smoother, rules-based valuation lowers import costs and uncertainty for trading, manufacturing and pharma importers.

sources.label: TBS – customs valuation reform
TBS – NBR surcharge net

items.title: Budget FY27: 'trillion-dollar dream' needs a smarter industrial policy
bullets: Commentary on the FY27 budget argues Bangladesh's long-run growth ambitions hinge on a sharper, more selective industrial policy rather than broad subsidies.
Post-budget optimism has been a key driver of the recent equity rally that pushed the DSEX past 5,600 for the first time in 9.5 months.
Sector implications: targeted incentives would favour export-oriented RMG, pharma and power names over protected, low-productivity industries.

sources.label: The Business Standard – Thoughts

items.title: Iran and US agree to halt war and reopen Strait of Hormuz; oil tumbles
bullets: Washington and Tehran agreed to halt hostilities and reopen the Strait of Hormuz after a month-long naval blockade, sending crude prices sharply lower; Trump says the deal is 'all signed'.
The strait carries about a fifth of global oil and gas; its reopening eases the supply-shock premium that had pushed Brent toward and above $100/bbl during the crisis.
Lower oil is a clear positive for energy-importing Bangladesh — easing fuel-subsidy, inflation and current-account pressure and supporting power, fuel and cost-sensitive sectors.

sources.label: TBS – Iran, US agree to halt war, reopen Hormuz
TBS – Trump says Iran deal 'all signed'

items.title: US Fed widely expected to hold rates at June 16-17 FOMC
bullets: Markets price ~99% odds the Federal Reserve keeps the funds rate at 3.50-3.75% at this week's FOMC, with new chair Kevin Warsh signalling patience.
Sticky, energy-driven inflation and unemployment near 4.4% keep the Fed in wait-and-see mode after its last cut in late 2025.
A steady Fed and softer oil give emerging markets like Bangladesh more policy space and reduce taka-depreciation pressure.

sources.label: Mitrade – Fed June 2026 decision

items.title: DSEX surges past 5,600 for first time in 9.5 months on post-budget optimism
bullets: Benchmark DSEX jumped 100.5 points (+1.8%) to close at 5,625, up from 5,520 — its first close above 5,600 since roughly mid-2025.
Turnover rose 9.7% to Tk 1,360 crore (Tk 13.6 billion) from Tk 1,240 crore, signalling broad participation.
Breadth strongly positive: of 396 issues, 243 advanced, 95 declined, 58 unchanged.
Driven by optimism over FY2026-27 budget incentives for the capital market; momentum likely to persist if BSEC reform delivers.

sources.label: BSS

items.title: Financial institutions and banks lead the charge; miscellaneous lone loser
bullets: Financial Institutions gained 4.4%, Banking 3.3% and IT 3.2% — the heaviest-weighted sectors powering the index higher.
General Insurance dominated turnover at 15.4% of traded value, followed by Pharmaceuticals and Banking at 11.9% each.
Miscellaneous was the only major decliner, down 3.5%.
Leadership by banks/NBFIs points to valuation re-rating of rate-sensitive financials on budget and reform hopes.

sources.label: BSS

items.title: Chattogram bourse mirrors Dhaka's rally
bullets: CSE Selective Categories Index (CSCX) rose 91.1 points; the All Share Price Index (CASPI) gained 147.6 points.
Confirms a market-wide, not Dhaka-only, response to the budget — supportive for sustained sentiment.

sources.label: BSS

items.title: Market enters the new week off a fourth straight weekly gain
bullets: Week ending June 11 closed DSEX at 5,520 (+45 on the week), DS30 at 2,073 and DSES at 1,067 — a fourth consecutive weekly advance.
Drivers: optimism over new BSEC leadership and expectations of market-friendly FY27 budget measures.
Today's breakout above 5,600 extends that trend into a fifth week.

sources.label: TBS

items.title: FY2026-27 budget widens listed-vs-unlisted tax gap, cuts merchant-bank tax
bullets: Corporate tax differential between listed and unlisted firms raised from 5% to 7.5%, a clear incentive to list.
Merchant banks get a reduced 27.5% rate (from 37.5%) to boost market-making and supply of quality shares.
Listed-company rate held at 22.5% (20% if all income routed via banking channels); unlisted at 27.5% (25% via banking channels).
BSEC chairman called the budget positive for the capital market.

sources.label: The Daily Star
TBS

items.title: Cabinet approves proposed FY2026-27 budget
bullets: The proposed national budget for FY2026-27 was approved at a cabinet meeting chaired by PM Tarique Rahman.
Historic inclusion of explicit capital-market strategies signals official recognition of the sector's economic role.
Sets the policy backdrop for the equity rally seen this session.

sources.label: The Daily Star

items.title: BSEC to be fully restructured within two weeks, says minister
bullets: Finance and Planning Minister said a new BSEC chairman and four commissioners will be appointed through a strictly professional, non-political process.
A credibly independent regulator is the key swing factor for attracting institutional and foreign inflows.
Reform agenda already includes Margin Rules 2025 and updated IPO, Mutual Fund and Public Issue rules.

sources.label: TBS
BSS

items.title: CSE welcomes FY27 budget, seeks deeper tax incentives
bullets: Chattogram Stock Exchange welcomed the budget's capital-market focus but pressed for further tax incentives to strengthen the market.
Suggests exchanges expect more supportive measures during budget passage.

sources.label: BSS

items.title: AGM and dividend season in full swing for Dec-2025 year-end firms
bullets: Numerous listed firms are disclosing FY2025 (year ended Dec 31, 2025) results with AGMs scheduled through June 2026.
Examples reported include 25% cash + 3% stock dividends, and EPS such as Tk 22.60 (NAV Tk 210.31) and Tk 2.02 (NAV Tk 33.85).
Record dates and AGM outcomes are near-term price catalysts for individual names.

sources.label: AmarStock DSE News

items.title: Regulatory scrutiny: DSE query over dividend-rule compliance
bullets: DSE has sought explanations from listed firms over dividend-rule breaches (e.g., Dulamia Cotton), part of tighter compliance enforcement.
Heightened surveillance aids governance but can pressure non-compliant small caps.

sources.label: TBS

items.title: Forex reserves climb to about $35.6 billion
bullets: Gross reserves stand near $35.63 billion, continuing a multi-month rebuild aided by strong remittances and moderated imports.
Government targets $51.7 billion in reserves by end-FY27.
Stronger reserves underpin taka stability and reduce external-financing risk — supportive for equities and importers.

sources.label: BSS
Dhaka Tribune

items.title: Bangladesh Bank holds policy rate at 10% amid sticky inflation
bullets: BB maintains a tight stance; rate cuts unlikely until inflation stays below 8% for several consecutive months.
Crawling-peg band modestly widened to improve forex-market efficiency and channel remittances formally.
Import cover near 3.5 months remains below the IMF's 6-month comfort threshold — a lingering vulnerability.

sources.label: Procapitas
Royal Capital (MPS)

items.title: Middle East tensions keep oil elevated, clouding global rate-cut path
bullets: Renewed Iran-US conflict has pushed crude sharply higher in 2026, with episodic 7% single-day surges.
Higher energy prices raise Bangladesh's import bill and inflation risk, pressuring the taka and reserves.
Hormuz supply fears are a key external risk for fuel, fertiliser and freight costs.

sources.label: Al Habtoor Research
FXStreet

items.title: Fed rate-cut odds fade as energy-driven inflation bites
bullets: Market-implied odds of a June 2026 Fed cut have collapsed (around 3-4%), with 'hold' the base case.
A higher-for-longer US rate path strengthens the dollar and pressures emerging-market currencies including the taka.
Tighter global liquidity can dampen foreign portfolio inflows into frontier markets like Bangladesh.

sources.label: Crypto Briefing

marketSnapshot.asOf: DSE close, Thursday, June 11, 2026 (last completed session; 2026-06-13 figures pending)
marketSnapshot.turnover: Tk 1,238 crore (Tk 12.4bn, up 2.4% on the session)

items.title: DSEX edges up to 5,520 on budget day as investors hunt value
bullets: In the final session of the week (Thu, 11 June) the DSEX rose 3.6 points to 5,520, recovering from a late-session dip on renewed buying in the final hour.
Turnover firmed 2.4% to ~Tk1,238 crore (Tk12.4bn); breadth stayed negative at 153 advancers vs 187 decliners, signalling selective rather than broad accumulation.
General Insurance led turnover (18.5%), then Engineering (11.4%) and Textile (10.3%); Miscellaneous (-4.1%) and Travel (-1.8%) lagged.
Impact: market is consolidating after a sharp multi-week rally; direction next week hinges on how investors digest the FY27 budget details.

sources.label: BSS — Stocks edge higher
TBS — Indices inch down as investors book profit

items.title: BSEC scraps floor price on Beximco, Islami Bank; both hit lower circuit
bullets: Regulator lifted the minimum-price cap on Beximco and Islami Bank shares to restore free-market pricing, ending a long-standing distortion.
Both scrips slumped to the lower circuit on the first floor-free sessions as pent-up selling pressure overwhelmed bids.
Impact: removes an artificial price prop — expect continued volatility in the two heavyweights and a cleaner read on true valuations.

sources.label: TBS — BSEC removes floor price on Beximco and Islami Bank
TBS — Beximco, Islami Bank hit lower circuit

items.title: Turnover hit a 22-month high on reform hopes around new BSEC team
bullets: Daily turnover spiked to a 22-month high earlier in the rally as investor confidence rebuilt around expected governance reforms.
The surge accompanied a 10-session DSEX winning streak before pre-budget profit-taking cooled momentum on 8 June.
Impact: liquidity is returning to the bourse — sustained turnover above Tk1,200-1,400 crore would support further re-rating if reform follow-through is credible.

sources.label: TBS — Turnover hits 22-month high on new BSEC hopes

items.title: New BSEC chair vows 'aggressive' push for direct listing of large firms
bullets: Appointment of a new BSEC chairman and commissioners drove a 70-point DSE surge on the announcement day as markets bet on stronger governance.
The new chief pledged an aggressive drive to bring big companies to market via direct listing to deepen the bourse.
Impact: a credible pipeline of quality listings plus tighter oversight could structurally widen the investable universe and lift institutional participation.

sources.label: TBS — BSEC chief vows aggressive push for direct listing
TBS — DSE index welcomes new BSEC chair with 70-point surge

items.title: Finance minister unveils Tk9.38 lakh crore FY27 budget
bullets: FM Amir Khosru presented a Tk9,38,000 crore budget for FY2026-27 on 11 June — the largest ever — built around recovery, jobs and reform.
Revenue target ~Tk6,95,000 crore (NBR tasked with ~Tk6,04,000 crore); fiscal deficit projected at 3.55% of GDP.
Impact: scale of spending is expansionary but execution and revenue mobilisation remain the key risks flagged by analysts.

sources.label: TBS — FM unveils Tk9.38 lakh cr budget for FY27
The Daily Star — Largest budget in the works
TBS — At a glance: FY2026-27 budget

items.title: Budget fine print: EV duty cuts, source-tax relief, black-money window for housing
bullets: Duties to be cut on electric and hybrid vehicles while taxes rise on petrol cars, nudging a green-mobility shift.
19 sectors may see lower AIT/source tax; FICCI and KCCI welcomed the Finance Bill 2026 as business-friendly.
An indemnity window would let undisclosed money flow into real estate if buyers declare true prices.
Impact: targeted relief is positive for autos, real estate and export-linked sectors; the black-money clause is politically sensitive but typically lifts property-linked stocks.

sources.label: TBS — Incentives for EVs, higher taxes on fossil-fuel cars
TBS — 19 sectors may see cuts in AIT, source tax
TBS — Black money allowed in real estate if true prices declared

items.title: Tk20,000 crore stimulus for distressed industries stirs speculative bets
bullets: Government floated a Tk20,000 crore package to revive closed and financially distressed industrial enterprises.
Speculative interest has surged in shares of non-operational or fundamentally weak listed firms on hopes of revival.
Analysts warn there is no clarity yet on eligibility, conditions, distribution or timing of disbursement.
Impact: caution warranted — recent price spikes in weak counters are expectation-driven and may not be sustainable.

sources.label: TBS — Indices inch down (stimulus details)

items.title: Standard Islami Bank director to receive 50.58 lakh shares as family gift
bullets: A Standard Islami Bank director is set to receive 50.58 lakh shares transferred as a family gift (off-market transmission).
Such intra-family transfers reshuffle sponsor holdings without affecting free float or triggering open-market supply.
Impact: neutral for price but worth tracking for sponsor-shareholding disclosures.

sources.label: TBS — Standard Islami Bank director to receive 50.58 lakh shares

items.title: Banglalink eyes Teletalk merger; parent Veon keen on Nagad
bullets: Banglalink signalled intent to merge with state operator Teletalk and form a partnership with BTCL.
Veon, Banglalink's parent, has expressed interest in investing in mobile-money operator Nagad.
Impact: potential consolidation could reshape the telecom and fintech landscape; watch for regulatory and valuation signals affecting listed telecom peers.

sources.label: TBS — Banglalink intends to merge with Teletalk
TBS — Veon expresses interest to invest in Nagad

items.title: Naser Ezaz Bijoy steps down as Standard Chartered Bangladesh CEO
bullets: Long-serving CEO Naser Ezaz Bijoy has resigned from Standard Chartered Bangladesh.
Leadership change at a top foreign bank comes amid a sector under heightened scrutiny over hidden losses.
Impact: limited direct market effect (StanChart is unlisted) but signals continued churn in banking-sector leadership.

sources.label: TBS — Naser Ezaz steps down from StanChart Bangladesh CEO role

items.title: Islami Bank under reserve pressure after Tk4,240cr withdrawals; BB sends observer
bullets: Islami Bank faces liquidity strain following ~Tk4,240 crore in withdrawals after a contested chairman appointment.
Bangladesh Bank appointed Ashraful Alam as an observer; depositors' forum staged sit-ins demanding the chairman's removal.
Impact: governance turmoil at the country's largest private bank is a near-term overhang on ISLAMIBANK shares and banking-sector sentiment.

sources.label: TBS — Islami Bank under reserve pressure amid withdrawals
TBS — BB appoints Ashraful Alam observer to Islami Bank

items.title: Bangladesh economy crosses the half-trillion-dollar mark
bullets: Bangladesh entered the $500 billion GDP club for the first time, a milestone for the South Asian economy.
The achievement underpins the budget's recovery narrative and longer-term consumption-growth story.
Impact: supportive backdrop for domestic-demand sectors (consumer, pharma, telecom) over the medium term.

sources.label: TBS — Bangladesh enters $500b GDP club for first time

items.title: Forex reserves rebuild to highest since late 2022
bullets: Bangladesh Bank's gross reserves climbed to about $34.06 billion, the highest since November 2022, aided by stronger remittances.
Gross reserves crossed $29 billion on the IMF (BPM6) calculation; BB bought ~$4.3 billion from the interbank market this fiscal year.
Impact: a healthier external buffer eases taka pressure and import financing, a positive for macro stability and importer-heavy sectors.

sources.label: The Daily Star — Forex reserves cross $29b on IMF method

items.title: Oil tumbles toward $89 as Trump pauses Iran strikes, Hormuz reopening eyed
bullets: Brent fell over 4% toward $89 (lowest since March) after President Trump suspended planned strikes on Iran and signalled a possible deal.
A draft 14-point agreement envisages lifting oil sanctions and reopening the Strait of Hormuz within 30 days; prices remain ~30% above pre-conflict levels.
World Bank lowered its global growth forecast citing the Iran war's impact.
Impact: lower crude eases Bangladesh's import bill, fuel-subsidy and inflation pressure — net positive for the taka, power/energy costs and overall equity sentiment if sustained.

sources.label: CNBC — Oil falls as Hormuz ship traffic increases
BSS — World Bank lowers global growth forecast on Iran war
TBS — Israel and Iran strike each other since ceasefire

items.title: US Fed seen holding rates as cut bets slip into 2027
bullets: Markets price near-certainty that the Fed holds at its June 2026 meeting, with inflation still above target and spending resilient.
Economists have pushed rate-cut expectations further out, with some surveys eyeing 2027.
Impact: a higher-for-longer US rate path keeps the dollar firm and pressures emerging-market flows and the taka — a headwind for frontier equities like the DSE.

sources.label: Yahoo Finance — Economists push Fed rate-cut expectations into 2027
Goldman Sachs — Why the Fed is unlikely to cut rates this year

marketSnapshot.asOf: DSE close, Monday, June 8, 2026 (last fully reported session; June 9 figures pending)

items.title: Beximco, Islami Bank crash to lower circuit on first floor-free session
bullets: First trading day without floor prices (effective 9 June): Beximco fell 9.99% to ~Tk99.1 and Islami Bank dropped 9.81% to ~Tk29.4 within minutes of the open.
Massive order imbalance — ~9.30 crore Beximco shares offered at the 9.99% discount with no buyers; ~16 lakh Islami Bank shares offered at 9.81% off with no bids — forced trading halts in both.

Impact: the unwinding of long-suspended floor prices (in place since 28 July 2022) is letting both heavyweights re-price toward market levels; expect continued circuit-limit moves and index drag until a clearing price is found.

sources.label: TBS — Beximco, Islami Bank hit lower circuit as selling pressure overwhelms market

items.title: DSEX snaps 10-day winning streak on pre-budget profit-taking (8 June close)
bullets: DSEX shed 33 pts (-0.61%) to 5,482; DS30 -18 to 2,069, ending an impressive 10-session rally as investors locked in gains ahead of the national budget.
Breadth bearish: 247 decliners vs 102 gainers, 44 unchanged; ~Tk1,600cr wiped from market cap in one session.
Turnover fell 30% to Tk1,072cr. Heavyweight drags: Square Pharma, Beximco Pharma, BRAC Bank, LafargeHolcim, BAT BC. General insurance led turnover (19.2%); Anwar Galvanizing topped gainers (+~10%).
CSE's CASPI also fell 84 pts to 15,314; turnover there -24% to Tk43cr.

sources.label: TBS — DSEX snaps 10-day winning streak amid pre-budget profit-taking

items.title: BSEC scraps last floor prices on Beximco and Islami Bank
bullets: At a special meeting on 8 June, BSEC withdrew the floor price on Beximco (last floor Tk110.10) and Islami Bank (Tk32.60), effective 9 June — the final two stocks under the regime imposed on 28 July 2022.
Completes a phased exit begun in January 2024; the DSE Brokers Association (DBA) credited the regulator with restoring a free market.
Impact: removes a long-standing distortion and should improve price discovery and index integrity over time, but triggers near-term sharp downside as suppressed prices reset.

sources.label: TBS — BSEC lifts minimum price cap on Beximco, Islami Bank stocks
BSS — DBA credits BSEC for lifting floor prices

items.title: New BSEC chief vows 'aggressive' push for direct listing of big firms
bullets: The newly installed BSEC chair pledged an aggressive drive to bring large, profitable companies to market via direct listing to deepen the bourse and improve quality of supply.
Sentiment around the leadership change had earlier driven turnover to a 22-month high and a 70-point welcome surge for the index.
Impact: credible reform signalling supports medium-term confidence, though the budget overhang and floor-price unwind are dominating near-term tape.

sources.label: TBS — BSEC chief vows 'aggressive' push for direct listing of big firms
TBS — Turnover hits 22-month high on new BSEC hopes

items.title: Islami Bank bleeds Tk4,240cr deposits; seeks Tk10,000cr BB liquidity
bullets: Deposits fell from Tk1,84,382cr (31 May) to Tk1,80,141cr (7 June) — a Tk4,240cr, seven-day drain — amid protests against the appointment of ex-BB deputy governor Khurshid Alam as chairman.
Current-account balance at the central bank reportedly slid from over Tk7,015cr to ~Tk2,600cr; the bank has requested Tk10,000cr in special liquidity support from Bangladesh Bank.
Impact: governance turmoil at the country's largest private bank pressures its share price (now floor-free) and feeds broader banking-sector risk sentiment.

sources.label: TBS — Islami Bank faces withdrawal pressure: Tk3,500cr taken out in 5 days
Dhaka Tribune — Islami Bank asks BB for Tk10,000cr liquidity support

items.title: Naser Ezaz Bijoy resigns as Standard Chartered Bangladesh CEO
bullets: Long-serving CEO Naser Ezaz Bijoy has stepped down from the helm of Standard Chartered Bangladesh, the country's leading foreign bank.
Leadership transition at a major trade-finance and custody player is watched closely by listed-market participants given StanChart's institutional footprint.
Impact: limited direct equity effect (SCB is unlisted locally) but signals continued churn in senior banking leadership.

sources.label: TBS — Naser Ezaz steps down from StanChart Bangladesh CEO role

items.title: Standard Islami Bank director to receive 50.58 lakh shares as family gift
bullets: A director of Standard Islami Bank (ex-Standard Bank) is set to receive 50.58 lakh shares transferred as a family gift, a non-market transmission disclosed to the exchange.
Such intra-family transfers do not change the sponsor block's overall holding but are routine disclosure events monitored for insider-ownership shifts.
Impact: neutral for float and price; informational for governance/ownership tracking.

sources.label: TBS — Standard Islami Bank director to receive 50.58 lakh shares as family gift

items.title: FY2026-27 budget: NBR may cut AIT/source tax across 19 sectors
bullets: Ahead of the FY2026-27 national budget, NBR is reportedly weighing cuts to advance income tax (AIT) and source tax across some 19 sectors to ease business costs.
Prior budgets sweetened the capital market via a wider listed/unlisted corporate-tax gap (to 7.5%), a lower brokerage-turnover AIT (0.05%→0.03%) and a reduced merchant-bank tax rate.
Impact: pre-budget caution is capping the rally; confirmation of market-friendly measures could re-ignite buying, while disappointment risks further profit-taking.

sources.label: TBS — 19 sectors may see cuts in AIT, source tax
The Daily Star — Bangladesh National Budget 2026-27

items.title: Reserves and remittances stay strong heading into FY26 close
bullets: Record remittance momentum: ~$2.98bn in the first 23 days of May (up ~41% YoY); cumulative FY26 receipts (through 23 May) hit ~$32.31bn vs ~$26.64bn a year earlier.
Gross reserves have climbed sharply — recently around $34bn (highest since Nov 2022 on gross basis) / above $29bn under the IMF (BPM6) method — aided by the strong inflows.
Taka broadly stable near ~Tk122.8/USD. Impact: a healthier external position supports macro stability, eases import pressure and underpins banking-sector liquidity.

sources.label: The Daily Star — Forex reserves cross $29 billion under IMF calculation
TBS — Bangladesh's forex reserves rise to $29.86b

items.title: Bangladesh Bank holds policy rate at 10% to anchor inflation
bullets: BB kept the policy (repo) rate at 10% for H2 FY26, maintaining a firm contractionary stance; SLF at 11.50% and SDF trimmed to 8.00%.
Inflation target for FY26 is 6.5%; headline had eased to ~5.7% by mid-2025 from 9%+ in 2023. A rate cut is contingent on real policy rate reaching ~3%.

NPL clean-up continues, with targets to push the ratio below 10% for state banks and 5% for private banks. Impact: higher-for-longer rates weigh on rate-sensitive banks and NBFIs but support taka stability.

sources.label: The Daily Star — BB holds policy rate at 10% in tough trade-off
BSS — BB holds policy rate at 10pc for H2 FY26

items.title: Oil whipsaws as Hormuz blockade and Israel-Iran flare-up roil markets
bullets: Crude fell to ~$87.7/bbl on 9 June (-3.93% on the day) after Brent had spiked above $98 earlier in the month; the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed under a US-Iran dual blockade, disrupting crude, fuel and LNG flows.
Israel and Iran struck each other for the first time since their ceasefire, keeping a geopolitical risk premium in the market despite weak Chinese demand (imports near 8-yr lows).
Impact for Bangladesh: elevated/volatile oil lifts the import bill and pressures the taka, power-sector subsidies and inflation; a sustained spike would be a clear negative for equities.

sources.label: TradingEconomics — Brent crude oil price
TBS — Israel and Iran strike each other for first time since ceasefire

items.title: Fed seen holding at 3.50-3.75% at June 16-17 FOMC
bullets: Markets expect no change at the 16-17 June FOMC, with the funds range steady at 3.50-3.75% amid above-target CPI and a resilient labour market (May payrolls +172k, unemployment 4.3%).
Some forecasters (e.g., Goldman Sachs) now see no 2026 cuts and possible removal of easing-bias language.
Impact: higher-for-longer US rates keep the dollar firm and pressure emerging-market currencies, including the taka, and can curb foreign portfolio appetite for frontier markets like Bangladesh.

sources.label: Federal Reserve — FOMC statement, April 29, 2026
Federal Reserve — FOMC meeting calendar

items.title: Distressed & low-priced stocks lead rally as revival-fund news lifts DSE
bullets: DSEX rose 35 pts to 5,442; DS30 +7 to 2,057; DSES +10 to 1,099. Turnover jumped to ~Tk1,279cr — the highest single day in eight months and up 18.43% on the session.
Breadth strongly positive: 243 advancers vs 98 decliners, 49 unchanged.

Top movers (mostly distressed/junk counters): Regent Textile +10%, Prime Finance +10%, Sonargaon Textiles +9.93%, Meghna Pet +9.89%, Bangladesh Industrial Finance +9.76%, Nurani Dyeing +9.68%, New Line Clothings +9.43%, Emerald Oil +9.18%.
Sector leadership: Services +3.7%, Paper & Printing +3.2%, Financial Institutions +2.9%.
Watch: rally is concentrated in speculative low-caps on revival-fund optimism — quality of the move is questionable; momentum can reverse fast.

sources.label: TBS — Distressed stocks surge as revival fund news fuels rally

items.title: SONARGAON: no undisclosed price-sensitive information behind price/volume spike
bullets: In response to a DSE query dated June 2, 2026, the company stated there is no undisclosed price-sensitive information explaining the recent unusual price hike and volume increase.
Implication: the run-up appears speculative — trade with caution.

sources.label: DSE — Today's News (display_news)

items.title: ACMELAB: shares of late Sponsor Director & MD transmitted to nominees
bullets: Shares held by the late Mr. Mizanur Rahman Sinha, Sponsor Director and Managing Director, were transmitted to the BO accounts of his nominees on June 3, 2026.
Governance note: succession at the sponsor/MD level — monitor for any subsequent board reconstitution announcements.

sources.label: DSE — Today's News (display_news)

items.title: GLOBALINS board meeting June 11 to consider FY2025 audited accounts
bullets: Global Insurance will hold a board meeting on June 11, 2026 at 3:00 PM to consider audited financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2025 (and likely a dividend recommendation).
Event to watch for EPS/NAV and dividend signal.

sources.label: DSE — Today's News (display_news)

items.title: FY2025 dividend & AGM calendar fills up for June–July
bullets: Multiple listed firms have recommended FY2025 (year ended Dec 31, 2025) dividends with AGMs clustering in June–July: 10% cash (AGM June 29), 15% cash (AGM June 29), 100% / Tk10 per share final cash (virtual AGM June 10), and 29% cash (AGM July 27).
SOUTHEASTB has set a record date for a stock dividend.
Action: confirm record dates before buying for dividend entitlement; ex-dividend price adjustments apply.

sources.label: LankaBD — Corporate events calendar
DSE — Last 7 days news (AmarStock mirror)

items.title: New BSEC chairman Masud Khan unveils capital-market reform roadmap
bullets: On June 4, 2026, newly-appointed BSEC chairman Masud Khan laid out a reform agenda focused on rebuilding investor confidence and moving toward an institution-driven emerging market.
Fair price discovery: no new floor prices will be imposed; existing floor mechanisms to be withdrawn gradually — a structurally important signal for free price movement.
Digitisation: IPO applications, rights issues, bond/sukuk approvals and licensing to shift to digital platforms.
Supply push: actively courting multinationals, state-owned enterprises and large local corporates to list.
Surveillance: real-time market-abuse detection system to be built with DSE, CSE and CDBL.

sources.label: BSS — BSEC chief unveils reform agenda
BSEC official site

items.title: Bangladesh Bank extends Tk200cr special capital-market fund to end-2026
bullets: Scheduled banks' special Tk200 crore capital-market investment fund — originally due to expire Feb 2025 — has its term extended to December 31, 2026.
Supportive for market liquidity; ties into the revival-fund optimism driving the current rally.

sources.label: Bangladesh Bank

items.title: Budget FY27: aggressive revenue target, tax tweaks that touch investors
bullets: Government is structuring a revenue target above Tk600,000 crore for FY27 — roughly a 20% jump — against a shortfall that has already breached Tk100,000 crore in the first nine months of FY26.
Under review: reinstating a 20% withholding tax on interest on foreign loans (previously withdrawn after pushback).
FBCCI opposes raising the corporate minimum tax from 0.60% to 1.00%.
Proposed VAT hike on online sales commission from 5% to 15% — negative for e-commerce names.
Higher taxes + elevated interest rates remain a drag on private investment.

sources.label: Dhaka Tribune — Budget FY27 tax net
The Daily Star — High taxes & rates stifle investment

items.title: Inflation seen dipping below 7% by mid-2026 on tight policy
bullets: Interim government expects headline inflation to fall below 7% by June 2026, supported by contractionary monetary policy and fiscal restraint.
On a 12-month average basis, inflation fell below 9% in November 2025 — the first time since June 2023.
Disinflation supports the case for eventual rate easing — a medium-term positive for equities, especially rate-sensitive financials.

sources.label: Financial Express — Inflation below 7% by mid-2026
Fibre2Fashion

items.title: Forex reserves strengthen; IMF support expanded to $5.5bn
bullets: Gross reserves rose to ~$35.1bn in April 2026 (from ~$34.1bn in March); reserves crossed $29bn on the IMF (BPM6) calculation.
Drivers: stable exchange rate, stronger remittance inflows and higher domestic interest rates curbing capital flight.
IMF has expanded combined program support to about $5.5bn — reinforcing external stability and reform momentum.

sources.label: The Daily Star — Reserves cross $29bn (IMF method)
The Daily Star — IMF expands support to $5.5b

items.title: Banking-sector stress: NPLs at record 30–36% under IMF reform watch
bullets: Non-performing loans have climbed to an estimated 30–36% of disbursed credit — among the highest ratios globally — keeping the banking sector under intense scrutiny.
Bangladesh remains bound by IMF conditionalities (ECF/EFF/RSF, ~$5.4–5.5bn) requiring tax-revenue benchmarks, exchange-rate flexibility and banking reform.
Sector read-through: pressure on weak banks' valuations; selective strength in well-capitalised names.

sources.label: Chambers — Banking Regulation 2026 Bangladesh

items.title: Oil shock: Strait of Hormuz disruption pushes Brent to ~$117 — import-cost risk for BD
bullets: Brent averaged ~$117/bbl in April 2026 (highest monthly average since June 2022) after a de facto closure of the Strait of Hormuz, through which ~20% of global oil supply flowed.
Bangladesh is a net energy importer — sustained high crude raises the import bill, pressures the taka and risks reigniting inflation, complicating the disinflation path.
Negative for fuel-intensive sectors (power, cement, transport, manufacturing margins); watch subsidy/fuel-price policy responses.

sources.label: EIA — Short-Term Energy Outlook (global oil)
IMF WEO April 2026 — Global economy in shadow of war

items.title: Fed flags energy risk; oil-importing Asia faces slower growth, stickier inflation
bullets: The IMF expects the oil-driven growth slowdown and inflation pickup to hit emerging Asia hardest given reliance on Middle East crude.

Fed officials highlight energy prices and supply-chain disruption as key risks; central banks watching for wage-price spirals and de-anchored expectations — a 'higher-for-longer' global rate backdrop pressures EM flows.
For Bangladesh: tougher external financing conditions and a stronger-dollar bias are headwinds for the taka and foreign portfolio sentiment.

sources.label: IMF WEO April 2026 (Ch.1 PDF)
IMF — WEO Spring Meetings 2026 briefing
